rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive hydrogenation/deuteration of unactivated alkyl alkenes using catalytic Ph3SiCl as an H/D-carrier and H2O/D2O as the H/D source, with sacrificial Fe anode and Ni-bipyridine co-catalyst
electrochemical_role: Cathodic two-electron reduction of Ph3SiCl (silicon mediator) generates a silyl anion that is protonated by H2O to give Ph3SiH; the sacrificial Fe anode releases Fe(n+) salts that, upon further reduction, form the active [Fe]-H species via sigma-bond metathesis with Ph3SiH

key_intermediates.name: linear alkyl-iron Fe-INT3A (major after migratory insertion)
silanol Ph3SiOH (D, regenerates Ph3SiX via fluorination)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ph3SiCl by two sequential SETs to silyl radical then silyl anion, which is protonated by H2O to Ph3SiH; sacrificial Fe anode dissolves to give Fe(n+), which after reduction undergoes sigma-bond metathesis with Ph3SiH to form [Fe]-H. [Fe]-H performs migratory insertion across the alkene, and the resulting alkyl-Fe is protonated by H2O to release the hydrogenated product and Fe-OH, closing both Si and Fe cycles.

probe_reagents.name: Ph3SiH (instead of Ph3SiCl)
probe_reagents.observation: Affords product in 73% yield, supporting silane as the operative H-carrier intermediate

probe_reagents.name: alkyl-SiMe3 (substrate 75)
probe_reagents.observation: No hydrogenation product; 86% substrate recovered, showing Si-Cl bond (not Si-C) is the activated bond

probe_reagents.name: PhMe2SiD / D2O
probe_reagents.observation: α and β positions deuterated (up to 85/79% D-inc.); two H atoms come from silane and from water; w/o NiBr2·dtbpy α-D drops (56/85), supporting Ni-assisted insertion

probe_reagents.name: ((1R,2S)-2-vinylcyclopropyl)benzene (76)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 77 (20%) and unopened hydrogenation product 78 (33%) detected, consistent with β-carbon elimination from a metal-alkyl intermediate (not free radical)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: >99% D-incorporation at terminal alkene carbons; chain-walking gives multi-site D-labeling in internal alkenes

probe_reagents.name: TMSCl / TMSBr (19F NMR monitoring)
probe_reagents.observation: Rapid conversion to TMSF via reaction with TBABF4, evidencing fluorosilane as a viable mediator state

probe_reagents.name: no electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed (>99% substrate recovered), confirming electrochemical activation is essential

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Ph3SiCl and Ni/L
probe_reagents.observation: Ph3SiCl reduces at Ep/2 = -2.4 V; Ni(II)/Ni(I) at -1.82 V and Ni(I)/Ni(0) at -2.55 V; addition of Ph3SiCl enhances Ni(0) generation

notes: Two interconnected catalytic cycles are proposed: a Si-mediator cycle (Ph3SiCl → Ph3Si• → Ph3Si- → Ph3SiH → Ph3SiX) and an Fe-hydride cycle that performs migratory insertion/protonation on the alkene. A minor parallel Ni-catalyzed cycle is also invoked (supported by ring-opening of vinylcyclopropane and Ni CV data) and detailed in SI. Heterogeneous hydrogenation on Fe-coated Ni foam contributes as well, since Zn(+) or Mg(+) anodes give product only when the cathode is pre-coated with Fe particles. Authors favor the [Fe]-H pathway over a free-radical pathway based on the radical-clock outcome (β-carbon elimination from alkyl-Fe rather than radical ring-opening).
